name: Gravel route between Burgo de Osma and the fortress of Gormaz
name_original: Ruta gravel entre el Burgo de Osma y la fortaleza de Gormaz
description:
More than a thousand years ago, battles were fought on the border between Muslims and Christians in the lands of the upper Duero. From those exploits, walled treasures remained, such as the Burgo de Osma or the caliphal fortress of Gormaz, the largest in Europe at its time.

With this simple tour along dirt tracks and secondary roads, we want to propose that you ride your gravel bike in the footsteps of the Castilian knight, Rodrigo Díaz de Vivar, El Cid, and contemplate from within the fort that he himself conquered. You will also cross the landscape of fields, with the largest apple orchard in Spain, and the Soria moor, cross the Duero over a medieval bridge and visit the beautiful Mozarabic hermitage—the Christians who lived under Muslim rule—of San Miguel.

The Route is circular from Burgo de Osma, a perfect town to give you a Castilian gastronomic tribute, which you can reach by road. The most recommended seasons to enjoy this adventure are spring and autumn, as long as you avoid rainy days and later, as some tracks get muddy. You will not find shadows on the itinerary, so it is advisable to bring sunscreen. It is also necessary that you carry provisions of water, food and spare parts because you do not pass through any town that has services.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma offers routes through varied terrain, characterized by river valleys and agricultural landscapes. The region features a mix of open fields, wooded sections, and paths alongside waterways like the Río Lobos. Elevation changes are generally moderate, with some routes including more significant climbs. Best gravel bike trails around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma The most popular gravel bike trail is Ucero – Río Lobos loop from Valdelinares, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 46 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the scenic Río Lobos area. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Ucero – Ucero loop from El Burgo de Osma - Ciudad de Osma, a moderate 27.7 miles (44.5 km) path. This route features a mix of open and wooded sections with moderate elevation gain. Local gravel bikers also love the San Esteban de Gormaz – San Esteban de Gormaz loop from San Esteban de Gormaz, a 16.9 miles (27.1 km) trail leading through the agricultural lands around San Esteban de Gormaz, often completed in about 2 hours 34 minutes. Gravel biking around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma is defined by river valleys, agricultural fields, and wooded areas. The network offers options for both moderate and challenging ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The gravel bike trails in this region primarily feature varied terrain, including river valleys and agricultural landscapes. You'll encounter a mix of open fields, wooded sections, and paths running alongside waterways like the Río Lobos. Elevation changes are generally moderate, though some routes do include more significant climbs.

Q: Are there any challenging gravel bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking a more demanding ride, the region offers at least one challenging route. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nufri Apple Orchards, La Rasa – Navapalos loop from El Pilón&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 60.8 km (37.8 miles) trail with over 550 meters of elevation gain, providing a substantial workout.

Q: Can I find long-distance gravel bike routes near Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: Absolutely. If you're looking for longer rides, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38152749" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nufri Apple Orchards, La Rasa – Navapalos loop from El Pilón&lt;/a&gt; covers nearly 61 km (37.8 miles). Another excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38151791" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero – Ucero loop from El Burgo de Osma - Ciudad de Osma&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 44.5 km (27.7 miles) long.

Q: Are the gravel bike routes in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma typically circular?
A: Yes, all the gravel bike routes highlighted in this guide are designed as loops. This means you'll generally start and finish at the same point, making logistics like parking and transportation straightforward. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero – Río Lobos loop from Valdelinares&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option.

Q: What kind of historical sites or natural landmarks can I see along the gravel bike trails?
A: The region is rich in both natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1072363" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cueva Grande (Río Lobos Canyon)&lt;/a&gt; or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3999886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Devil's Eye (Natural Rock Arch)&lt;/a&gt;. For history enthusiasts, there are impressive castles like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2054152" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gormaz Castle&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/294710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero Castle&lt;/a&gt;, many of which are accessible from or visible along various routes.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly gravel bike trails in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: While there are no routes explicitly classified as 'easy' in terms of overall effort, several moderate trails are suitable for beginners with a reasonable level of fitness. These routes often feature less technical terrain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40004473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Esteban de Gormaz – San Esteban de Gormaz loop from San Esteban de Gormaz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 27.1 km (16.9 miles) ride that offers a good introduction to gravel biking in the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for gravel biking in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma, with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the open agricultural areas, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable. Winter can bring colder temperatures and potentially wet conditions, but routes remain accessible for those prepared.

Q: Are there any gravel bike routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in the region traverse varied landscapes that offer scenic views, particularly along river valleys and from elevated sections. The trails around the Río Lobos area, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40003217" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucero – Río Lobos loop from Valdelinares&lt;/a&gt;, are known for their natural beauty and opportunities for picturesque vistas of the canyon and surrounding nature.

Q: How many gravel bike routes are available in this guide for Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: This guide features 5 curated gravel bike routes around Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma, offering a range of experiences from moderate to challenging, all designed to showcase the diverse landscapes of the region.

Q: What do other komoot users say about gravel biking in Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma?
A: Over 10 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Burgo De Osma-Ciudad De Osma's varied terrain. While specific ratings are not yet available, users frequently praise the region's mix of open fields, wooded sections, and paths alongside waterways, highlighting the diverse and engaging riding experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly gravel cycling options in Burgo De Osma?
A: For families looking for a less strenuous ride, the moderate routes are generally suitable, provided children have some cycling experience and stamina. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40004467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Esteban de Gormaz – San Esteban de Gormaz loop from San Esteban de Gormaz&lt;/a&gt;, at 27.9 km (17.3 miles), offers a relatively flat profile through agricultural lands, which can be a good option for a family outing.
tips: El Burgo de Osma, then called Uxama, suffered for centuries under alternating domination and reconquest between the Moors and the Spaniards. While there is no historical evidence that the Spanish knight and national hero El Cid played a role in Osma's story during this period, it cannot be ruled out either, as he was the lord of the castle and landlord of neighboring Gormaz.
Rolling along a wide dirt road, we have splendid views of Burgo de Osma. An ideal place to practice mountain biking.
To get to the castle you have to climb a steep ramp, but needless to say, it is worth every pedal stroke to see this fabulous medieval fortress.
Under the imposing gaze of the castle of Gormaz, we find this beautiful image of the hermitage of San Miguel. It is worth visiting if we visit the castle.

Formation of rocks of capricious shapes. Legend has it that they are the horses of a group of Muslim warriors turned to stone by the miracle of a princess converted to Christianity who prayed to God for her salvation when she was persecuted. Before, the myth was much more "credible", but a few years ago when the cart was enlarged, part of the rock formation was thrown away.

Navapalos is mentioned in the Cantar de Mio Cid as the place where the Cid Campeador and his men crossed the Duero river. After camping near the town, the poem tells that the archangel Gabriel appeared to the Cid and wished him good luck.

The town has beautiful traditional adobe architecture, as well as an interesting Islamic watchtower.
Near Burgo de Osma, the Camino del Cid runs along this beautiful and quiet road under an impressive limestone wall and close to the riverbed of the Ucero.
